name: Pierogi
recipeInstructions:
1. Mound the flour on a clean surface and make a well in the center.
2. Pour the eggs into the well and cut them with a knife.
3. Gradually add salt and water, kneading until the dough is firm.
4. Cover the dough with a warm bowl and let it rest for 10 minutes.
5. Divide the dough into two halves and roll out each half to a thin sheet.
6. Cut circles using a large biscuit cutter.
7. Place a small mound of filling on one side of each circle of dough.
8. Moisten the edge with water, fold over, and press firmly together.
9. Ensure the pierogi are well sealed to prevent the filling from leaking out.
10. Gently drop the pierogi into salted boiling water.
11. Cook for 3 to 5 minutes or until they float to the surface.
12. Carefully lift the cooked pierogi out of the water using a perforated spoon.
Note: Pierogi can be frozen after boiling and stored well. They are often fried in butter and onions seasoned with salt and pepper before serving. Avoid overcrowding when frying; fry on medium-low heat until golden brown. Varieties of filling include cheese, cabbage and mushrooms, sauerkraut, mushrooms, meat, prunes, etc., as described in the original instructions.
